news.summary: EU diplomats are travelling to Ukraine to mark the fourth anniversary of the massacre in the city of Bucha. It occurred only weeks into the war: after Russia evacuate the town, returnees found hundreds of bodies of men, women, children and elderly people who had been mass executed. FRANCE 24's Dougl

news.headline: Middle East war impacts world tourism as travelers consider trip choices
news.summary: The ongoing war in the Middle East is already having an impact on tourism around the world. Airlines from the region have been severely impacted, and holiday makers are now opting for destinations away from the theatre of war.

news.headline: Arab World Faces ‘Profound’ Economic Crisis From Iran War, U.N. Agency Warns
news.summary: An economic simulation warned that the region’s economy could lose more than $190 billion in just one month, and that Gulf states that have often bankrolled reconstruction efforts will be less able to help.

news.headline: China pledges ‘strategic coordination’ with Pakistan to help end US war on Iran
news.summary:
China said on Tuesday it would enhance “strategic coordination” with Pakistan on the Iran crisis to promote dialogue and help end the conflict as Islamabad’s top diplomat Ishaq Dar arrived in Beijing.
Dar’s trip, his second to China in three months, came just days after a phone call with Chinese For

news.headline: Schools across Sindh and Balochistan to re-open from April 1, have classes 5 days a week
news.summary:
<p>Schools in Sindh and Balochistan will resume classes from April 1 (Wednesday) and follow a five-day working week amid the fuel crisis, the provincial governments said on Tuesday.</p>

news.headline: Trump Faces a Decision on Whether to Start a Ground War in Iran
news.summary: The president wants a negotiation, but the Iranians say they are refusing until a cease-fire is declared. And while Marines and the 82nd Airborne Division offer new leverage, the risks escalate quickly.
